title: New Timeline Picker doesn't have Milestone choice
excerpt: When I opened my boards this morning, I noticed the new Timeline Picker doesn’t have the option to set the chosen date as a milestone anymore. How can I set a date as a milestone now for my Gantt charts?

posts.author: mgross (Marissa Gross)
posts.cookedHtml: When I opened my boards this morning, I noticed the new Timeline Picker doesn’t have the option to set the chosen date as a milestone anymore. How can I set a date as a milestone now for my Gantt charts?

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
It looks like the new Timeline Picker no longer includes the option to set a milestone directly. I can still see it on my account at the bottom.

However, you can still set a milestone in your Gantt chart by doing the following:

  1. Open your board and navigate to the item where you want to set a milestone.
  2. Go to the Timeline or Date column associated with your Gantt chart.
  3. Set the start and end date to be the same—this will automatically display the item as a milestone in the Gantt view.
  4. Check your Gantt chart to confirm that the milestone appears as a diamond icon.
    image441×325 31.7 KB

posts.author: mgross (Marissa Gross)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unfortunately, your directions are inaccurate.

image290×421 18.9 KB

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml: Sorry not sure why my version of Monday.com is different, have you tried asking support?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: Looks to be that the new timeline column simply does not support the “set as milestone” options

posts.author: JMehrer (Jamie Mehrer)
posts.cookedHtml: This is no longer working for me either. I am emailing support now.

posts.author: JMehrer (Jamie Mehrer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Support got back to me. You have to go to the Settings for the Timeline column - Customize Timeline - and select Show “Set as Milestone” and it will give you the option on the date picker.

image332×379 18.2 KB

posts.author: mgross (Marissa Gross)
posts.cookedHtml: You are a hero! Thank you!!!
